classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

title: That God is Not Defiled by Our Sins.

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: And yet, though all things that He established are in Him, those who sin do not defile Him, of whose wisdom it is said: "She touches all things by reason of her purity, and nothing defiled assails her."

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: For it behooves us to believe that as God is incorruptible and unchangeable, so also is He consequently undefilable.
